turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="barGrad" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#FFD56A"/>
      <stop offset="0.55" stop-color="#E7B848"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#C9972C"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="shine" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#FFF2B8" stop-opacity="0"/>
      <stop offset="0.45" stop-color="#FFF2B8" stop-opacity="0.55"/>
      <stop offset="0.7" stop-color="#FFF2B8" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="sunGrad" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="60%">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#FFF1A6"/>
      <stop offset="0.6" stop-color="#FFC94A"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#E2A72B"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- Sun / golden glow -->
  <g transform="translate(256 145)">
    <circle r="58" fill="url(#sunGrad)" stroke="#D39A1F" stroke-width="4"/>
    <!-- Rays -->
    <g stroke="#E6B23A" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.95">
      <line x1="0" y1="-105" x2="0" y2="-145"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="105" x2="0" y2="145"/>
      <line x1="105" y1="0" x2="145" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="-105" y1="0" x2="-145" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="74" y1="-74" x2="104" y2="-104"/>
      <line x1="-74" y1="-74" x2="-104" y2="-104"/>
      <line x1="74" y1="74" x2="104" y2="104"/>
      <line x1="-74" y1="74" x2="-104" y2="104"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Gold ingot -->
  <g>
    <rect x="135" y="220" width="242" height="172" rx="22" fill="url(#barGrad)" stroke="#B9801E" stroke-width="6"/>
    <!-- Edge shading -->
    <rect x="135" y="220" width="242" height="172" rx="22" fill="none" stroke="#F8D777" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.6"/>
    <!-- Highlight strip -->
    <rect x="200" y="220" width="62" height="172" rx="12" fill="url(#shine)" opacity="0.75"/>
    <!-- Indent details -->
    <path d="M165 270 C190 255, 322 255, 350 270" fill="none" stroke="#A56E16" stroke-width="4" opacity="0.6"/>
    <path d="M165 315 C190 300, 322 300, 350 315" fill="none" stroke="#A56E16" stroke-width="4" opacity="0.45"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Coins peeking around -->
  <g opacity="0.98">
    <ellipse cx="105" cy="310" rx="44" ry="32" fill="#F2C24E" stroke="#B9801E" stroke-width="5"/>
    <ellipse cx="105" cy="310" rx="31" ry="22" fill="#E2A72B" opacity="0.9"/>
    <ellipse cx="405" cy="285" rx="46" ry="34" fill="#F2C24E" stroke="#B9801E" stroke-width="5"/>
    <ellipse cx="405" cy="285" rx="33" ry="24" fill="#E2A72B" opacity="0.9"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Small sparkle dots -->
  <g fill="#FFF1B8" opacity="0.9">
    <circle cx="250" cy="125" r="4"/>
    <circle cx="286" cy="160" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="220" cy="170" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="160" cy="260" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="356" cy="268" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="325" cy="380" r="3"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating gold illustrations**

I need to draw representations of "gold" without using any letters. I think using shapes like a gold bar and a coin would be effective! Maybe I'll create an ingot shape with sun rays adding some flair. I definitely want to provide this in SVG format. I envision crafting a centered gold bar featuring a gradient, along with circular coins. It’s interesting to focus purely on visual elements without text!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
